title: fix(gen): validate chat image model capabilities
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Rejects chat requests with `image_url` parts when the requested model does not support image input
- Runs validation globally after model resolution, before image fetching or upstream calls
- Adds focused tests for text-only and vision-capable models

Validation:
- `./node_modules/.bin/biome check gen.pollinations.ai/src/text/generateTextPortkey.ts gen.pollinations.ai/src/text/transforms/validateInputModalities.ts gen.pollinations.ai/test/text/transforms/validateInputModalities.test.ts`
- `../node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit`
- `../node_modules/.bin/tsx --eval ...` smoke test

Note: local Vitest could not start because Rollup's native optional dependency is blocked by macOS code signing in this environment; CI should run the normal test path.

gist.headline: No pictures, please
gist.blurb: We've added a quick vision check to our chat endpoints. If you try to show a picture to a text-only model, it'll now politely let you know instead of getting confused.
gist.summary: Chat requests containing `image_url` parts are now explicitly rejected during validation if the requested model does not support image inputs.
gist.impact: Developers will receive immediate, clear validation errors when sending images to text-only models, saving time and preventing confusing upstream failures.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a helpful inspector, sorting text documents from photographs to ensure the right data goes to the right place.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny inspector glasses, holding a clipboard and sorting glowing text documents from pixelated photographs. A chunky retro terminal in the background displays a green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
